trail.val: HBM sales to triple y/y in 2026
HBM4 mass production Feb; 'fully booked'; HBM4 >50% of HBM sales from Q3; HBM sales >3x y/y in 2026
real possibility Samsung overtakes SK Hynix as #1 HBM supplier in 2027

forward.note: HBM4 catch-up vs SK Hynix is the central debate; 1c yield resolution the bull/bear fault line.

trail.val: Techknowledge checks: 1c HBM4 test yields ~50-60%, ramp 'on track'
SemiAnalysis: 1c HBM4 test-wafer yields remain 'extremely low'; volume only later in 2026

forward.note: Directly conflicting checks on the key proof point; riskier swing jumping straight to 1c DRAM.

trail.val: Jefferies (Jeff Kim) Q2-26 OP KRW 100tn vs his prior KRW 84tn
union-settlement provision (~12% of profit) hits Q2, ~2pts above JPM's prior 10%

2Q26 PRELIMINARY OP W89.4tn (beat lowered Street W75-84tn after ~W15tn+ 1H labor provisions), sales ~W171tn; memory OPM ~80% DRAM / ~65% NAND; 4nm HBM4 base-die in full MP. Stock fell ~7% on the print (hurdle raised); full detail Jul 30
trail.src: Samsung prelim / JPM Jay Kwon (OW W480k)

forward.note: Jefferies' KRW 100tn OP not quite hit but a clear beat; the stock sold off — expectations, not results, now the binding constraint.

trail.val: HBM4 price still unfinalized despite Samsung already shipping; expected +90-100% vs last year's LTA

forward.note: De-commoditization optionality via custom base die (Samsung's in-house foundry edge).

trail.val: largest DRAM cap ~740K wfr/mo end-2026 (~180K HBM); only +45K in 2027; P5L ph.1 mid-2028, ph.2 ~2030

forward.note: Clean-room space the binding constraint; 2027 supply growth 'very limited' -> supports pricing.

trail.val: utilization up; 2nm GAA ramp in Q4
Taylor TX move-in Apr 23, ops 2026 / mass production 2027; 1.4nm on track; BSPDN target 2027
Samsung Foundry signed deal to make 'Claros' power-management chips for AI data centers

Foundry backlog ~KRW50tn (~$32.7B); Anthropic 2nm win reportedly agreed (+DeepX), Taylor completed Tesla's A15/AI5 tape-out (2nd US 2nm chip), and Samsung to fab Google's 2nm 'Icefish' TPU I/O die — but JPM/Gokul caps Samsung+Intel at ~5-6% of 2028-29 2nm (overflow, not leadership)

forward.note: Foundry valued at zero-to-negative EV; the accumulating AI-lab/hyperscaler wins are real availability-driven overflow, capped near-term by TSMC's ~90-95% 2nm dominance.

trail.val: NAND->DRAM conversion drives global NAND wafer cap -3% in 2026; Samsung losing NAND share as it prioritizes DRAM/HBM

forward.note: Capex diverted to DRAM/HBM; supportive of NAND pricing but Samsung cedes share.
